name: Sentinel-1 (SAR)
description: Sentinel-1 is a C-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellite mission providing all-weather, day-and-night radar imaging for land and ocean monitoring. It is the backbone for flood detection under adverse weather conditions.
purpose: All-weather, day-and-night radar imaging of Earth's surface for land monitoring, maritime surveillance, and emergency response.
observations: Ocean surface wind and wave data
access_mechanism: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem (CDSE) API, Copernicus Browser, Alaska Satellite Facility (ASF)
strengths: Operates independently of weather conditions -- penetrates clouds, rain, and fog
Day-and-night operation capability
High spatial resolution (5m x 20m in IW mode)
Excellent for detecting water surfaces due to SAR specular reflection
6-day revisit time (constellation of two satellites)
Free and open data policy
Well-established flood mapping workflows (thresholding, change detection)
limitations: Cannot provide spectral information (no vegetation health or water quality)
Interpretation requires SAR-specific expertise
Affected by speckle noise requiring filtering
Urban and mountainous areas can produce geometric distortions (layover, foreshortening)
Single polarisation limits some classification tasks

detection.explanation: SAR is the gold standard for flood detection. Water surfaces appear dark in SAR imagery due to specular reflection, enabling reliable flood extent delineation even under cloud cover.

extent_assessment.explanation: High-resolution SAR imagery allows precise mapping of flood extent. Change detection between pre- and post-event images provides accurate flood boundary delineation.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: C-band SAR operates at microwave frequencies that penetrate clouds, rain, and fog completely. This is the primary advantage of Sentinel-1 for flood monitoring.

night_operations.explanation: SAR is an active sensor providing its own illumination. It operates identically during day and night, making it indispensable for 24/7 flood monitoring.

emergency_response.explanation: Sentinel-1 is routinely activated during flood emergencies by Copernicus EMS. Its all-weather capability makes it the first resource consulted during crisis events.

name: Sentinel-2 (Optical Multispectral)
description: Sentinel-2 is a high-resolution optical multispectral imaging mission providing systematic global observations of land surfaces with 13 spectral bands, ideal for flood extent mapping and vegetation/water analysis in clear-sky conditions.
purpose: High-resolution optical multispectral land monitoring for agriculture, forestry, land cover change detection, and disaster mapping.
observations: Multispectral imagery (13 bands, visible to SWIR)
True colour and false colour composites
access_mechanism: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem (CDSE) API, Copernicus Browser, Google Earth Engine, Microsoft Planetary Computer
strengths: Very high spatial resolution (10m visible, 20m red-edge/SWIR)
13 spectral bands enable water body detection using NDWI/MNDWI indices
5-day revisit time (constellation)
Intuitive visual interpretation -- true colour images are easy to understand
SWIR bands allow flood detection even under vegetation
Free and open data policy
Excellent for post-flood damage assessment and recovery monitoring
limitations: Cannot acquire usable images through clouds -- optical sensor is cloud-dependent
No night-time imaging capability -- requires solar illumination
Atmospheric correction needed for accurate analysis
Limited use during prolonged cloudy/rainy periods typical of flood events

detection.explanation: Water indices (NDWI, MNDWI) derived from multispectral bands provide excellent flood detection in clear-sky conditions with high spatial detail.

extent_assessment.explanation: 10m resolution provides detailed flood extent maps. SWIR bands can detect water beneath vegetation canopy. Visual products are intuitive for emergency managers.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: Optical sensors cannot penetrate clouds. During active flood events with heavy rain and cloud cover, Sentinel-2 imagery is often unusable.

night_operations.explanation: Passive optical sensor requires solar illumination. Cannot acquire imagery during night-time.

emergency_response.explanation: Valuable when clear skies allow acquisition. Often used in combination with Sentinel-1 for comprehensive flood damage assessment after cloud clearance.

name: Sentinel-3 (Ocean & Land Monitoring)
description: Sentinel-3 is a multi-instrument mission carrying optical, altimetry, and thermal sensors. It provides wide-swath land and sea surface monitoring useful for large-scale flood overview and water level measurements.
purpose: Global land and ocean monitoring including sea surface temperature, ocean colour, land surface conditions, and inland water level measurements via altimetry.
access_mechanism: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem, EUMETSAT Data Store, Copernicus Online Data Access (CODA)
strengths: Very wide swath (1270 km for OLCI) enables near-daily global coverage
Radar altimeter measures river and lake water levels
Multiple sensors (optical, thermal, altimetry) on single platform
Useful for large-scale flood extent overview
Thermal bands can detect waterlogged soil
Free and open data policy
limitations: Lower spatial resolution (300m-1km) compared to Sentinel-1/2
Optical instruments affected by cloud cover
Not suitable for detailed local-scale flood mapping
Altimetry limited to tracks (not continuous spatial coverage)
temporal_resolution: Less than 2 days (constellation), 27-day repeat cycle for altimetry

detection.explanation: Wide swath provides overview of large-scale flooding. Altimetry can detect abnormal river water levels, serving as flood early warning indicator.

extent_assessment.explanation: Useful for regional-scale flood extent estimation, but resolution is too coarse for detailed local mapping.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: OLCI optical sensor is affected by clouds. However, SRAL radar altimeter operates in all weather conditions for water level monitoring.

night_operations.explanation: SLSTR thermal bands and SRAL radar altimeter operate during night-time. OLCI optical bands require daylight.

emergency_response.explanation: Provides rapid wide-area overview during large flood events. Altimetry data supports flood early warning systems.

name: Sentinel-5P (Atmospheric Monitoring)
description: Sentinel-5P carries the TROPOMI spectrometer for atmospheric composition monitoring. While primarily designed for air quality monitoring, it provides aerosol and cloud information that can indirectly support flood monitoring operations.
purpose: Global monitoring of atmospheric composition including trace gases (NO2, O3, SO2, CO, CH4, HCHO), aerosols, and cloud properties.
observations: Tropospheric NO2, O3, SO2, CO columns
Aerosol Index and Aerosol Layer Height
Cloud fraction, cloud top height, cloud optical thickness
access_mechanism: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem, Sentinel-5P Expert Users Data Hub, Google Earth Engine
strengths: Daily global coverage with 5.5 km x 3.5 km resolution
Provides cloud property information useful for planning optical acquisitions
Can detect methane emissions from flood-damaged infrastructure
Aerosol data helps assess atmospheric conditions during emergencies
Free and open data policy
limitations: Not designed for direct flood detection or mapping
No surface imaging capability
Atmospheric products require specialised interpretation
Coarse spatial resolution for surface-level applications
Indirect relevance to flood monitoring only

detection.explanation: Cannot directly detect floods. However, cloud fraction data can help identify areas where optical satellite acquisition is not possible.

extent_assessment.explanation: Not suitable for flood extent mapping. The sensor measures atmospheric columns, not surface features.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: Provides cloud cover maps that help operational planning -- identifying when and where optical sensors can acquire usable data.

night_operations.explanation: TROPOMI requires solar backscatter for most products. Limited night-time capabilities.

emergency_response.explanation: Can detect post-flood methane emissions or air quality changes. Cloud maps support mission planning for other sensors during emergencies.

name: Copernicus Emergency Management Service (EMS)
description: Copernicus EMS provides on-demand mapping products and early warning systems for natural disasters including floods. It is the primary European service activated during flood emergencies, producing rapid damage assessment maps.
purpose: Provide geospatial information derived from satellite and in-situ data for emergency management, including rapid mapping and early warning for natural disasters.
observations: Flood delineation, grading, and impact assessment maps
access_mechanism: EMS Rapid Mapping portal (activation request), EFAS web portal, GFM data access via WMS/API
strengths: Authoritative, validated flood mapping products
Rapid response -- first products within hours of activation
Combines multiple satellite sources (SAR, optical) for best results
EFAS provides probabilistic flood forecasts up to 10 days ahead
GFM provides continuous near-real-time flood monitoring globally
Products directly usable by civil protection authorities
Free for authorised users (national civil protection agencies)
limitations: Rapid Mapping requires formal activation -- not always-on
Activation is limited to authorised users (government agencies)
Processing time can range from hours to days
Coverage depends on satellite data availability at time of event
Historical archive limited to activated events
spatial_resolution: Variable -- depends on source imagery (typically 1.5m-10m for rapid mapping products)

detection.explanation: GFM provides continuous automated flood detection. Rapid Mapping produces expert-validated flood delineation maps upon activation.

extent_assessment.explanation: Rapid Mapping products include detailed flood extent, depth estimation, and affected area calculations -- the most authoritative flood extent products available.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: EMS utilises SAR data (primarily Sentinel-1) for mapping during cloudy conditions. GFM is based on Sentinel-1 SAR data.

night_operations.explanation: Service operates 24/7. SAR-based products can be generated from night-time acquisitions without any limitation.

emergency_response.explanation: This IS the primary European emergency response service for floods. Designed specifically for crisis situations with rapid delivery timelines.

name: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS)
description: CLMS provides pan-European and global land cover, land use, and biophysical parameter products. Its water-related products (EU-Hydro, Water Bodies) provide baseline hydrological data essential for flood monitoring context.
purpose: Systematic monitoring of the land surface to provide geospatial data on land cover, land use, vegetation, water bodies, and terrain for environmental policy support.
observations: CORINE Land Cover (CLC) and CLC+ products
EU-Hydro -- river network and catchment database
High Resolution Water and Wetness layers
access_mechanism: CLMS web portal, WMS/WFS services, direct download, Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem
strengths: Provides baseline water body and river network data (EU-Hydro)
High Resolution Water and Wetness layers help identify flood-prone areas
Pan-European coverage with consistent methodology
Riparian Zone mapping identifies flood-vulnerable areas
Land cover data supports flood damage assessment
Free and open data policy
limitations: Products are periodic (not real-time) -- typically annual or multi-year updates
Not designed for real-time flood event monitoring
Resolution varies by product (10m to 100m)
Water body products may not capture dynamic flood conditions

detection.explanation: Not designed for real-time flood detection. However, baseline water body data helps distinguish permanent water from flood water.

extent_assessment.explanation: EU-Hydro and High Resolution Water layers provide reference baseline for comparing pre- and post-flood water extent.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: Products are generated from multi-temporal composites but are not designed for real-time cloud-independent monitoring.

night_operations.explanation: Products are pre-generated maps, not real-time observations. Night-time operation is not applicable.

emergency_response.explanation: Provides critical context data -- baseline water bodies, land cover, and river networks used as reference layers during emergency response.

name: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem (CDSE)
description: CDSE is the unified access platform for all Copernicus Sentinel data and Copernicus Services products. It provides a single entry point for discovering, visualising, and downloading EO data from multiple sources.
purpose: Provide a single access point for discovery, visualisation, processing, and download of all Copernicus Earth Observation data and products.
observations: Copernicus Services products (EMS, CLMS, CAMS, CMEMS, C3S)
Jupyter notebook environment for data analysis
access_mechanism: OData API, OpenSearch, STAC API, Sentinel Hub, Copernicus Browser (web GUI), JupyterLab
strengths: Single platform for all Copernicus data -- simplifies data discovery
Multiple access methods (API, browser, processing environment)
Free registration and data access
Built-in visualisation and processing capabilities
STAC-compliant catalogue for interoperability
Integrated cloud processing environment
limitations: Requires registration for API access and downloads
Processing quotas for free-tier users
Platform-specific APIs require learning curve
Some historical data may have retrieval delays (cold storage)
temporal_resolution: Near real-time data ingestion for Sentinel missions

detection.explanation: Provides immediate access to Sentinel-1 SAR data for flood detection. Integrated tools allow quick visualisation and analysis.

extent_assessment.explanation: Access to both SAR and optical data enables comprehensive flood extent assessment combining multiple data sources.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: Provides access to cloud-independent SAR data. Users can quickly switch between optical and SAR sources based on weather conditions.

night_operations.explanation: Platform operates 24/7. SAR data acquired during night passes is available with minimal latency.

emergency_response.explanation: Fastest access to the latest Sentinel acquisitions. Processing environment allows rapid analysis during emergencies.

name: EUMETSAT H SAF (Hydrology SAF)
description: The EUMETSAT Satellite Application Facility on Support to Operational Hydrology and Water Management (H SAF) provides satellite-derived products for precipitation, soil moisture, and snow monitoring -- critical inputs for flood forecasting.
purpose: Develop and distribute satellite-derived products for operational hydrology including precipitation estimates, soil moisture, and snow parameters.
observations: Soil moisture products (surface and root zone)
Snow cover and snow water equivalent products
access_mechanism: H SAF product navigator, FTP data access, EUMETCast dissemination, EUMETSAT Data Store
strengths: Near real-time precipitation monitoring from geostationary satellites
Soil moisture products indicate pre-event saturation (flood predisposition)
Continuous monitoring (geostationary satellite updates every 15 minutes)
Products specifically designed for hydrological applications
Complements ground-based rainfall measurements
Supports flood forecasting models
limitations: Spatial resolution coarser than SAR/optical satellites (3-25 km)
Precipitation estimates have uncertainty compared to ground gauges
Not designed for direct flood extent mapping
Some products require EUMETSAT registration
Product availability varies by region

detection.explanation: Precipitation and soil moisture data are key early warning indicators. Extreme rainfall combined with saturated soils signals imminent flood risk.

extent_assessment.explanation: Coarse resolution limits direct flood extent mapping. Products are better suited for regional flood risk assessment.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: Microwave-based soil moisture and precipitation products operate through clouds. Geostationary thermal/IR products provide estimates even in cloudy conditions.

night_operations.explanation: Geostationary satellite monitoring operates continuously 24/7. Microwave products are independent of solar illumination.

emergency_response.explanation: Near real-time precipitation monitoring supports emergency flood forecasting. Soil moisture pre-conditions help assess flood severity potential.

name: EUMETView (Real-Time Satellite Viewer)
description: EUMETView is EUMETSAT's web-based platform for visualising near real-time satellite imagery from Meteosat, Metop, and other EUMETSAT satellites. It provides quick situational awareness of weather patterns and surface conditions.
purpose: Web-based visualisation of near real-time satellite products from EUMETSAT's fleet for weather monitoring, environmental observation, and situational awareness.
access_mechanism: Web browser (no registration required for basic viewing), WMS services
strengths: Immediate access -- no registration required for viewing
Near real-time imagery (updated every 15 minutes from Meteosat)
Quick situational awareness of weather and surface conditions
Multiple satellite sources in one viewer
WMS layers can be integrated into GIS platforms
Useful for assessing cloud cover before tasking optical sensors
limitations: Low spatial resolution (1-3 km from geostationary orbit)
Primarily a visualisation tool -- limited analytical capabilities
Not designed for quantitative flood mapping
Data download options are limited compared to data portals
Image interpretation requires meteorological expertise
spatial_resolution: 1 km (HRV channel), 3 km (standard channels) for Meteosat

detection.explanation: Resolution is too coarse for direct flood detection. However, can identify large-scale flood-related cloud systems and precipitation patterns.

extent_assessment.explanation: Not suitable for flood extent mapping due to coarse resolution. Useful only for very large-scale continental flood overview.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: Thermal/IR channels can see through thin clouds. Provides real-time cloud cover information essential for planning SAR/optical acquisitions.

night_operations.explanation: IR and thermal channels provide continuous imagery day and night from geostationary orbit. Updated every 15 minutes.

emergency_response.explanation: Provides immediate situational awareness of weather conditions during emergencies. Helps coordinate sensor acquisition planning.

name: Global Flood Awareness System (GloFAS)
description: GloFAS is the global component of the Copernicus Emergency Management Service providing probabilistic flood forecasts for large river basins worldwide. It combines weather forecasts with hydrological modelling for early warning.
purpose: Provide probabilistic flood forecasts and monitoring for large river basins globally, supporting early warning and preparedness for flood events.
observations: Probabilistic flood forecasts (up to 30 days ahead)
access_mechanism: GloFAS web portal, Copernicus Climate Data Store (CDS) API, WMS services
strengths: Extended-range probabilistic flood forecasts (up to 30 days)
Global coverage of major river basins
Based on ECMWF weather forecasts -- world-leading numerical weather prediction
Supports early warning and preparedness planning
Historical reanalysis for understanding flood risk
Freely accessible forecast products
limitations: Spatial resolution limited to major river basins (~5-10 km grid)
Not suitable for local/urban flood monitoring
Forecast uncertainty increases with lead time
Does not provide direct satellite observations
Model-based -- accuracy depends on input data quality
Flash floods in small catchments may not be captured
temporal_resolution: Daily forecasts (updated twice daily), 6-hourly discharge fields

detection.explanation: GloFAS forecasts detect upcoming flood events before they occur. Real-time monitoring detects when river discharge exceeds flood thresholds.

extent_assessment.explanation: Flood hazard maps provide estimated flood extent based on return periods, but resolution is coarse for detailed local assessment.

cloudy_conditions.explanation: Forecast-based system -- not dependent on satellite imagery acquisition. Operates independently of cloud cover conditions.

night_operations.explanation: Model-based forecasting operates continuously 24/7 regardless of illumination conditions.

emergency_response.explanation: Core early warning system for floods in Europe and globally. Provides lead time for emergency preparedness and evacuation planning.
